text: ↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡🇺🇸🇮🇷 U.S. President Donald Trump said he expects the U.S. economy to grow this year despite what he described as a temporary diversion caused by military operations against Iran. I had to go and fight — some people would call it a…" ⚡ 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 U.S. President Donald Trump said he views the conflict with Iran as "very close to over," adding that if U.S. forces withdrew now, "it would take them 20 years to rebuild that country." He also noted that the U.S. has received "zero" support from NATO throughout the operation. @wfwitness

They're…" ⚡ 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 U.S. President Donald Trump said he expects the U.S. economy to grow this year despite what he described as a temporary diversion caused by military operations against Iran. "I had to go to a war. I had to go and fight — some people would call it a war, some people call it a military operation. It was easy, because we have the greatest military in the world," Trump said, adding that he acted to prevent Iran from obtaining a nuclear weapon. "If I didn't do that, right now you would have Iran with a nuclear weapon," he said. @wfwitness

"Amazing 2 Days" Trump Teases Deal as Iran Threatenes Gulf U.S. President Donald Trump said Iran talks could resume, telling the world to watch out for "amazing 2 days" as US forces claim enforcement of a naval blockade on Iranian ports. Iran has also threatened total blockade of Gulf port activity if the U.S. insists on naval blockade. READ MORE

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡🇺🇸🇨🇳🇮🇷 U.S. President Donald Trump said Chinese President Xi Jinping wrote him a letter asserting that China is not supplying weapons to Iran, in response to a letter Trump said he sent after receiving reports of such transfers. "I wrote him a letter asking…" ⚡ 🇺🇸 🇨🇳 U.S. President Donald Trump declined to outline a specific response to a recent FBI-attributed Chinese cyber attack, saying "it is what it is" and acknowledging that both countries conduct cyber operations against each other. "China's China. They're never easy, but we're doing great with China," he said. Trump pointed to his 100% tariff on Chinese cars as evidence of his hardline stance, contrasting the U.S. approach with Europe, which he said is "being inundated with Chinese cars." @wfwitness

He added that Qatar, Saudi Arabia and the UAE were shocked when they were…" ⚡ 🇺🇸 🇨🇳 🇮🇷 U.S. President Donald Trump said Chinese President Xi Jinping wrote him a letter asserting that China is not supplying weapons to Iran, in response to a letter Trump said he sent after receiving reports of such transfers. "I wrote him a letter asking him not to do that, and he wrote me a letter saying that essentially he's not doing that," Trump said. Trump added that a major meeting with Xi is scheduled for next month, and that he does not believe the oil and Iran dynamic will affect it, describing Xi as "somebody I get along with very well." @wfwitness

The Corps paid approximately 250 million yuan to gain control of the satellite system. https://www.ft.com/world tweet

↩️ Reply to DD Geopolitics: "🇮🇷🇺🇸 The United States and Iran have reached a “principle agreement” to extend their current ceasefire until April 22 to continue negotiations, according to the Associated Press, citing sources. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics | Socials | Donate | Advertising" 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 The Iranian Foreign Ministry representative did not confirm media reports about an agreement with the US to extend the ceasefire. The US must prove the seriousness of its intentions to end hostilities. They have repeatedly disrupted the negotiation process, the Iranian Foreign Ministry stated. The Iranian Foreign Ministry stated that the date of the second round of negotiations with the US has not been scheduled yet, and called the views put forward by the US during the first round of negotiations in Pakistan unreasonable and unrealistic. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics
🇬🇧 🇺🇸 ⚡️ Starmer to Trump: “I Will NOT Yield!” UK Refuses to Join Iran War Despite UK-US Trade Deal Threat

⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇬🇧 🇮🇷 🇷🇺 US President Trump posts a headline from The Sun on TruthSocial: The report by defence editor Jerome Starkey argues that Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s rhetoric on defence is not matched by Britain’s military capabilities. The piece highlights concerns over reduced manpower, ageing equipment, and delays in defence investment, warning the UK is not prepared for major conflicts involving countries like Iran or Russia. It concludes that “words don’t win wars,” stressing the need for concrete military readiness amid rising global tensions. @wfwitness
